notes: contentKind must be 'guide' — this routes the importer to the Guides collection, not News.
lede (hero subhead) is auto-set from excerpt during import. Write excerpt as 1-2 punchy sentences that also work as a hero subhead.
body word count target is ~1,500 words. Guides are evergreen pillar content, not news.
medicalReviewer is required for treatment-guide and expert-qa categories.
relatedTreatmentSlug must match an exact treatment slug in the DB (botox, lip-filler, sculptra, etc.).
sourcesCount is auto-computed from body.sources during import. Aim for 8-10 sources — FDA, peer-reviewed studies, ASPS, academic medical centers, consumer health publishers.
readTimeMin is not set by the importer — leave it out of generated content.
seo.searchIntent tells the AI what funnel stage and search behavior to write for (e.g. Informational / TOFU). Not stored in DB — editorial planning metadata only.
seo.aeoTargets lists the specific AI Overview / featured snippet boxes the page should win (e.g. definition box, cost table, FAQ). Not stored in DB — editorial planning metadata only.
Inline links within body paragraphs are NOT supported by the importer — the importer builds plain Lexical text nodes. Use body.internalLinks for related-page links instead.

Sections must cover: what it is, how it works, what it treats (FDA-approved uses + off-label), how long results last, cost, risks and side effects, vs other options in its class, what to expect at the appointment, how to choose a provider.

items.title: REQUIRED: SEO-friendly guide title used as the page H1
items.excerpt: REQUIRED: 1-2 sentences, under 200 characters. Doubles as the hero subhead (lede). Should hook the reader and state what the guide covers.
items.coverImageAlt: REQUIRED: plain-language image description for accessibility

medicalReviewer.name: REQUIRED for treatment-guide and expert-qa: reviewer full name matching MedicalReviewers collection

items.relatedTreatmentSlug: REQUIRED: exact slug from treatmentSlugs list above, e.g. botox

seo.metaTitle: REQUIRED: usually same as title, under 70 characters if possible
seo.metaDescription: REQUIRED: 120 to 160 character search description, not identical to excerpt.
seo.focusKeyword: REQUIRED: primary keyword, e.g. 'what is botox'
secondaryKeywords: secondary keyword 1, e.g. how does botox work
secondary keyword 2, e.g. how long does botox last
entities: treatment name, brand name, active ingredient, regulator, study, or institution name
seo.searchIntent: REQUIRED: funnel stage + intent type, e.g. 'Informational / TOFU — pillar entry point for the treatment hub'
aeoTargets: REQUIRED: specific AI Overview or featured snippet box to win, e.g. 'definition box for what is [treatment]'
REQUIRED: second AEO target, e.g. 'cost table / how much does [treatment] cost'
REQUIRED: third AEO target, e.g. 'FAQ rich results / how long does it last'

body.answerSnippet: REQUIRED: 40 to 80 word answer-first summary placed at the top of the guide for featured snippets. Should directly answer the implied search query.
introParagraphs: REQUIRED: opening paragraph that states what the treatment is, who it is for, and why this guide exists.
REQUIRED: second paragraph with context — how common it is, who typically seeks it, or what readers will learn.

sections.heading: REQUIRED: What is [Treatment], exactly? — use a question-style H2 phrased as the target search query
paragraphs: REQUIRED: define the treatment clearly. What class of product it is (neuromodulator, filler, biostimulator, etc.), what its active ingredient does, and the brand vs. generic distinction if relevant.
REQUIRED: note if the brand name is commonly used as a generic term. Name the main competitors in the same class so search engines understand the entity landscape.

sections.heading: REQUIRED: How does [Treatment] work?
paragraphs: REQUIRED: mechanism of action in plain language — what happens at the biological level (muscle relaxation, volume addition, collagen stimulation, etc.).
REQUIRED: contrast with what it does NOT do, to set accurate expectations and distinguish it from alternatives.

sections.heading: REQUIRED: What does [Treatment] treat?
paragraphs: REQUIRED: list all FDA-approved cosmetic indications clearly. Include the specific anatomical areas and the approval dates for recent additions.
REQUIRED: list common off-label uses if well-established in clinical practice. Frame as 'experienced injectors also use it off-label for' — never as approved uses.

sections.heading: REQUIRED: How fast does it work — and how long does it last?
paragraphs: REQUIRED: onset timeline (e.g. 3-5 days to notice, 2 weeks for full effect). Source this from clinical data.
REQUIRED: duration range (e.g. 3-4 months). List factors that affect longevity: metabolism, dosage, area treated, muscle strength.

sections.heading: REQUIRED: How much does [Treatment] cost?
paragraphs: REQUIRED: pricing model (per unit vs. per syringe vs. flat fee). Give a realistic US price range with a named source (ASPS, RealSelf, etc.).
REQUIRED: explain what drives the price up or down: provider credentials, geography, number of units/syringes needed, clinic type.

sections.heading: REQUIRED: Is [Treatment] safe? Side effects and who should avoid it
paragraphs: REQUIRED: common side effects (bruising, swelling, redness) with frequency if known from clinical data. When to contact a provider.
REQUIRED: rare but serious risks. If there is an FDA boxed warning, state it accurately. Who should not receive this treatment (contraindications, allergies, pregnancy, neuromuscular conditions).
REQUIRED: downtime note — most people can return to normal activities when?

sections.heading: REQUIRED: [Treatment] vs other [options in its class]
paragraphs: REQUIRED: compare to 2-3 main alternatives in the same product class (e.g. Botox vs Dysport vs Daxxify for neuromodulators; Juvederm vs Restylane for fillers). Cover formulation differences, onset, spread, and duration.
REQUIRED: clarify that units are NOT interchangeable between brands. Which choice is right depends on the area, goal, and provider recommendation.

sections.heading: REQUIRED: What to expect at a [Treatment] appointment
paragraphs: REQUIRED: walk through the visit step by step: consultation (history, goals, muscle mapping), prep (numbing cream, skin cleaning), the procedure (needle size, injection pattern, duration — typically 10-15 min for neurotoxins).
REQUIRED: what happens right after: redness, swelling timeline, when to expect first signs of results, two-week follow-up norm.

sections.heading: REQUIRED: How to choose a [Treatment] provider
paragraphs: REQUIRED: what credentials matter — board-certified dermatologist or plastic surgeon, or trained nurse injector under medical supervision. What a good consultation looks like (units disclosed, per-unit rate given, no upsell pressure).
REQUIRED: one natural mention of injector.world as a directory to find and compare verified providers. Not promotional — frame as a resource.

atAGlance: REQUIRED: short fact 1, e.g. treatment type and target area.
REQUIRED: short fact 2, e.g. procedure time.
REQUIRED: short fact 3, e.g. typical results duration.

faq.question: REQUIRED: question phrased exactly as a user would type it, e.g. 'Does [treatment] hurt?'
faq.answer: REQUIRED: concise factual answer in 1-3 sentences. No promotional language. Claim must be backed by a source in body.sources.

faq.question: REQUIRED: age / candidacy question, e.g. 'At what age should I start [treatment]?'
REQUIRED: frequency / maintenance question, e.g. 'How often can I get [treatment]?'
REQUIRED: comparison question, e.g. '[Treatment] or [alternative] — what is the difference?'
faq.answer: REQUIRED: concise factual answer. Link to the comparison guide using the internalLinks field, not inline.

sources.title: REQUIRED: peer-reviewed study or clinical trial title
sources.publisher: REQUIRED: journal name, e.g. Dermatologic Surgery, JAMA Dermatology
claimsSupported: REQUIRED: exact claim this source backs, e.g. 'results appear in 3-5 days and last 3-4 months'

sources.title: REQUIRED: FDA prescribing info, medication guide, or approval announcement
claimsSupported: REQUIRED: exact FDA-approved indication or safety warning this source supports

sources.title: REQUIRED: American Society of Plastic Surgeons statistics or guidelines
claimsSupported: REQUIRED: exact stat supported, e.g. 'average provider fee ~$528'

sources.title: REQUIRED: academic medical center patient education page
sources.publisher: REQUIRED: Cleveland Clinic, Mayo Clinic, StatPearls (NCBI), or equivalent

sources.title: REQUIRED: manufacturer prescribing info or HCP page
REQUIRED: consumer health publisher page
sources.publisher: REQUIRED: WebMD, Healthline, Cleveland Clinic Health Essentials, etc.

sources.title: REQUIRED: pricing or market data source
REQUIRED: additional peer-reviewed or clinical reference

internalLinks.label: Find a qualified injector near you

body.disclaimer: This guide is for general information only and does not constitute medical advice. Consult a licensed provider before pursuing any aesthetic treatment.

about.name: REQUIRED: brand name of the drug/product, e.g. Botox (onabotulinumtoxinA)
REQUIRED: procedure name, e.g. Botox injection (cosmetic)
